channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Pope thanks volunteers for 'miracle of tenderness' as another Holy Year event passes without him
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis issued a message Sunday thanking volunteers for the “miracle of tenderness” they offer the sick, as he continued his recovery from double pneumonia and doctors reported some positive news: After more than three weeks in the hospital, the 88-year-old pope is responding well t
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: They were forced to scam others worldwide. Now thousands are held in detention on the Myanmar border
item.description: Thousands of sick, exhausted and terrified young men and women, from countries all over the world squat in rows, packed shoulder to shoulder, surgical masks covering their mouths and, some, their eyes.
item.dc:creator: Huizhong Wu, Jintamas Saksornchai And Martha Mendoza, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Johnson says Zelensky has done 'about face' on mineral deal
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said during a radio interview that Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has done a complete “about face” on the U.S.-Ukraine minerals deal that was not signed after the heated meeting between the two delegations late last month.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: A man with a Palestinian flag who climbed London's Big Ben tower is arrested
item.description: A man has been arrested after climbing the Big Ben tower at the Palace of Westminster in central London while barefoot and holding a Palestinian flag.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Macron's diplomatic comeback: from France's domestic crisis to reshaping Europe's defense
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron is back at the center of global diplomacy, seeking to ease relations with President Donald Trump, championing a Ukraine peace plan alongside his British counterpart, and seeing his longstanding desire to boost European defense turning into reality.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Honduras holds primaries as voter frustration simmers over security and the economy
item.description: Voters in Honduras will select candidates from the three main parties to compete in November’s general election for the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Marlon González, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rohingya refugees in Bangladesh brace for upcoming food reductions as aid agencies cut funding
item.description: COX’S BAZAR, Bangladesh (AP) — Rohingya refugees in crammed Bangladeshi camps say they are worried about a U.S.
item.dc:creator: SHAFIQUR RAHMAN and JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran's supreme leader rejects nuclear talks with US after Trump's overtures
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran's supreme leader, said in a series of X posts Saturday he would not negotiate a nuclear deal with the U.S. due to its demands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 12 people injured by 3 men shooting randomly at Toronto pub customers
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — A dozen people were injured in a shooting at an eastern Toronto pub in what police called a reckless act of violence by three men who entered the bar and fired randomly without warning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: In his own words: Pope Francis' views on resigning changed over time
item.description: ROME (AP) — As he recovers from pneumonia in the hospital, Pope Francis has increasingly handed off his day-to-day responsibilities to cardinals as questions swirl about the near and long-term future of his papacy.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis shows 'slight improvement' during fourth week in hospital, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis has apparently shown "slight improvement" and is responding will to oxygen therapy, the Vatican said on Saturday. This is the pope's fourth week at Rome's Gemelli Hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's Scottish golf resort vandalized by pro-Palestine group over Gaza stance
item.description: President Trump’s Turnberry golf resort in Scotland was vandalized with graffiti by pro-Palestine activists who are protesting his plans for Gaza and to displace Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's impeached President Yoon released from prison
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s impeached conservative President Yoon Suk Yeol was released from prison on Saturday, a day after a Seoul court canceled his arrest to allow him to stand trial for rebellion without being detained.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian strike on Ukraine kills more than 10 after Trump threatens sanctions
item.description: Russia struck the Donetsk region in Ukraine on Friday, killing at least 11 and wounding 30 people according to Ukraine’s officials, hours after President Trump threatened to impose sanctions on Moscow in an effort to drive ceasefire negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: 1 dead and several injured as tropical low tracks west across Australian east coast
item.description: BRISBANE, Australia (AP) — Flooding rains are set to continue lashing the Australian east coast even though it avoided the destructive winds of its first tropical cyclone in 51 years, officials said Saturday. One person was confirmed dead and several were injured.
item.dc:creator: JOHN PYE and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea's impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol freed from prison after canceled arrest
item.description: South Korea’s impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol was released from prison on Saturday after a court canceled his arrest to allow him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 22 dead amid gang clashes in Ecuador's largest city
item.description: Gang infighting has left nearly two dozen people dead in Guayaquil, Ecuador's largest city. 14 people were arrested in the aftermath of the Thursday skirmish.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Clashes in Syria between government forces and Assad loyalists kill more than 200
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Fighters siding with Syria's new government stormed several villages near the country's coast, killing dozens of men in response to recent attacks on government security forces by loyalists of ousted President Bashar Assad, a war monitor said.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia attacks Ukraine's energy supplies as US cuts its access to satellite images
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia attacked Ukrainian energy facilities with dozens of missiles and drones during the night, officials said Friday, hobbling the country’s ability to deliver heat and light to its citizens and to power weapons factories vital to its defenses.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 3 Bulgarians convicted of spying for Russia across Europe from base in UK
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Three Bulgarian nationals based in Britain were convicted Friday by a London jury of spying for Russia on what police said was “an industrial scale.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump seeking new nuclear deal with Iran with letter to supreme leader
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he has written a letter to Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, calling on the country to negotiate a new nuclear deal with the United States.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Far-right front-runner in Romania’s annulled presidential race files his candidacy in the rerun
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romanian far-right populist Calin Georgescu filed his candidacy Friday in a rerun of last year's presidential election that was annulled by a top court after his surprise first-round win.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Takeaways from the EU's landmark security summit after Trump said Europe must fend for itself
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders are trumpeting their endorsement of a plan to free up hundreds of billions of euros to inject into their defense budgets after the Trump administration warned that the continent must look after its own security, including Ukraine, in future.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hostages freed from Gaza tell Trump he was 'sent by God' to save them, but dozens more remain
item.description: President Donald Trump met with hostages freed from Hamas captivity in the Oval Office as his administration works to free the remaining 59 hostages still held in Gaza, including Edan Alexander.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy confirms Ukraine will attend US peace talks in Saudi Arabia, 1 week after Oval Office clash
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has confirmed that his team will participate in peace talks with their US counterparts in Saudi Arabia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aid efforts in Gaza slow as millions of promised USAID dollars do not arrive, agency officials say
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Trump administration’s cuts to USAID have frozen hundreds of millions of dollars in contractual payments to aid groups, leaving them paying out of pocket to preserve a fragile ceasefire, according to officials from the U.S. humanitarian agency.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN says 186 missing and at least 2 dead after migrant boats capsize off Yemen and Djibouti
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — At least two people have died and 186 others are missing after four boats carrying migrants from Africa capsized overnight in waters off Yemen and Djibouti, the U.N.'s migration agency said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans press Trump to resume military, intelligence aid to Ukraine
item.description: Republican lawmakers are starting to urge President Trump to reverse his decisions to pause U.S. military and intelligence assistance to Ukraine, warning that a prolonged stoppage of U.S. help for the war effort would have a seriously detrimental effect.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: EU leaders commit to working together after Trump signals that Europe must defend itself
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders on Thursday committed to working together to bolster the continent's defenses and to free up hundreds of billions of euros for security after U.S. President Donald Trump’s repeated warnings that he would cut them adrift to face the threat of Russia alone.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT and LORNE COOK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former Olympic snowboarder now on FBI's 10 most wanted list
item.description: Former Olympic snowboarder Ryan Wedding was added to the FBI’s 10 most wanted list Thursday for allegedly running a transnational drug trafficking network.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Mother of Abbey Gate victim says Biden ‘robbed’ family of justice
item.description: "If we can take one terrorist off the list of harming Americans, especially our soldiers, then that is a good day," Paula Knauss Selph told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas disregards Trump’s ‘last warning’ over hostage release
item.description: Hamas' response is an apparent dismissal of President Donald Trump's "last warning" issued on social media.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Schools closed, public transport halted as rare cyclone approaches Australia
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Alfred is forecast to cross the Queensland state coast somewhere between the Sunshine Coast region and the city of Gold Coast to the south early Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN PYE and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kabul airport bombing suspect 'will die in prison': Sebastian Gorka
item.description: Sharifullah is charged in federal court in Virginia with providing material support to a foreign terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: Kremlin says a 2022 Ukrainian decree bans Zelenskyy from talks with Putin
item.description: Russia on Wednesday asked how Ukraine could attend potential talks on ending their three-year war when a Ukrainian decree from 2022 rules out negotiations with President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope increases physical therapy, marks start of Lent from hospital
item.description: During the morning he participated in an Ash Wednesday celebration, receiving ashes and Holy Communion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope stable, but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
